context: clean-room: guard+probe only; runtime_provider; no tools
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in the unification of Italy, 1815–1871. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
1815 — Congress of Vienna
1815 — Joachim Murat's expedition in Italy
1815 — Battle of Tolentino
1820–1821 — Revolution in the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies
1821 — Revolt in Piedmont
1831 — Revolutions in Modena, Parma, and Romagna; Ciro Menotti
1831 — Giuseppe Mazzini founds Young Italy
1848 — Revolution in Sicily and Naples
1848 — Five Days of Milan
1848 — Charles Albert of Sardinia declares war on Austria
1848 — Republic of Venice declared
1848 — Pius IX elected Pope
1849 — Roman Republic proclaimed; Giuseppe Mazzini
1849 — Giuseppe Garibaldi defends the Roman Republic
1849 — Battle of Novara; Charles Albert abdicates in favor of Victor Emmanuel II
1849 — Fall of the Roman Republic to French forces
1849 — Fall of the Republic of Venice to Austria
1858 — Meeting at Plombières (Cavour and Napoleon III)
1859 — Franco-Austrian War (Second Italian War of Independence)
1859 — Battles of Magenta and Solferino
1859 — Armistice of Villafranca
1860 — Annexation of Tuscany, Parma, Modena, and Romagna
1860 — Garibaldi's Expedition of the Thousand; conquest of Sicily
1860 — Garibaldi conquers Naples
1860 — Battle of Volturno
1860 — Plebiscites in southern Italy and Sicily
17 March 1861 — Proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy at Turin; Victor Emmanuel II king
1865 — Florence becomes capital of the Kingdom of Italy
1866 — Third Italian War of Independence (Austro-Prussian War)
1866 — Battle of Lissa
1866 — Treaty of Vienna; Italy acquires Venetia
1870 — Capture of Rome (Porta Pia), 20 September
1870 — Plebiscite in Rome and the surrounding territories
1871 — Rome becomes capital of the Kingdom of Italy
